description: Upwind sampling position at the Agricultural Research, Development and Education Center (ARDEC) near Fort Collins, Colorado, used to characterize background aerobiome before exposure to agricultural activities.
Downwind sampling position at ARDEC near Fort Collins, Colorado, used to measure the aerobiome influence of livestock and crop agricultural operations.
SASS high-volume air sampler deployment site in Big Spring, Texas, positioned in semi-arid rangeland to characterize aerobiome of the southern Great Plains.
Mini SASS lightweight air sampler deployment at Big Spring, Texas, enabling comparison of high-volume and compact aerobiome collection methods in semi-arid conditions.
Upper sampling position on the flux tower at the Central Plains Experimental Range (CPER) in Nunn, Colorado, capturing aerobiome at height above the shortgrass steppe surface.
Lower sampling position on the flux tower at CPER, measuring the near-surface aerobiome of the shortgrass steppe to quantify vertical gradients in microbial community composition.
General environment sampling across the Central Plains Experimental Range in Nunn, Colorado, including soil, plant, and ambient air samples from the shortgrass steppe.
Northern rooftop sampling station on the CSU Foothills Campus Atmospheric Chemistry building, Fort Collins, used to monitor urban-influenced aerobiome at the mountain-plains interface.
Southern sampling station near the Regional Innovation Campus (RIC) on the CSU Foothills Campus, Fort Collins, providing a contrasting urban aerobiome measurement point.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Acadia National Park, Maine, providing eastern deciduous forest aerobiome data as part of the national long-term comparison network.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Everglades National Park, Florida, capturing subtropical wetland aerobiome for comparison with Rocky Mountain sites.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Grand Canyon National Park, Arizona, sampling aerobiome in arid canyon terrain of the Colorado Plateau.

site_name: IMPROVE — GRSM (Great Smoky Mountains National Park)
description: IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Great Smoky Mountains National Park on the Tennessee-North Carolina border, capturing aerobiome in temperate deciduous forest.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Hawai'i Volcanoes National Park on the Big Island, providing a tropical island aerobiome baseline far removed from continental sources.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, the closest IMPROVE site to CSU and a key reference for montane aerobiome composition.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Olympic National Park, Washington, capturing temperate rainforest aerobiome on the Pacific coast for continental comparison.
IMPROVE network aerosol monitoring station at Voyageurs National Park, Minnesota, capturing boreal forest and lake-dominated aerobiome of the northern Great Lakes region.
Upper elevation sampling position at Niwot Ridge Long-Term Ecological Research (NWT) site, Colorado, capturing high-alpine tundra aerobiome above treeline.
Mid-elevation sampling position at Niwot Ridge LTER, Colorado, characterizing the subalpine forest-tundra ecotone aerobiome along an elevation gradient.
Lower elevation sampling position at Niwot Ridge LTER, Colorado, measuring montane forest aerobiome as the baseline for the altitudinal transect.
Incubation experiment area at the Plant Growth Facility (PGF) on the CSU campus, Fort Collins, used for controlled aerobiome exposure studies.
Greenhouse sampling area at the CSU Plant Growth Facility, Fort Collins, capturing aerobiome within controlled plant growth environments.
Eastern sampling position at the Shortgrass Steppe Research Center (SGRC) in Nunn, Colorado, capturing aerobiome on the eastern perimeter of the primary grassland research area.
Southern sampling position at the Shortgrass Steppe Research Center (SGRC) in Nunn, Colorado, providing directional contrast for aerobiome source attribution studies.
General environment sampling across the Shortgrass Steppe Research Center (SGRC) in Nunn, Colorado, including soil and plant matrices in addition to air filters.
Animal habitat sampling area at the CSU Pueblo Turtle Farm research station, focused on aerobiome associated with reptile and wildlife enclosures for OneHealth pathogen dispersal research.
Environmental sampling area surrounding the CSU Pueblo Turtle Farm, capturing background aerobiome of semi-arid southern Colorado for comparison with animal enclosures.

Field sampling sites across North and South Carolina collected as part of the BROADN Fragmented Landscape project, characterizing aerobiome of southeastern US mixed forest and agricultural landscapes.
Sampling site at Doane University in Crete, Nebraska, a BROADN partner institution collecting aerobiome data from tallgrass prairie and agricultural landscapes of the central Great Plains.
Sampling area at the CSU Weed Research Facility near Fort Collins, Colorado, used to characterize aerobiome in agricultural research plots focused on invasive plant management.
Small controlled-environment aerosol chamber in the Hernandez lab at CSU, Fort Collins, used for experimental manipulation of aerobiome under defined atmospheric conditions.
Large controlled-environment aerosol chamber in the Hernandez lab at CSU, Fort Collins, enabling scaled aerobiome experiments including bioaerosol generation and fate studies.
